vulnerability.reference: Quality-based ordering that prioritizes stylistic conformity over content diversity reduces representation of minority and technical text.
vulnerability.generated: Curriculum learning orders training data by a single quality score, inherently clustering similar sequences without diversity-preserving constraints.

impact.reference: Ordered training regime causes models to overfit to narrow style, degrading generalization and harming performance on diverse tasks.
impact.generated: Homogenized training sequences degrade model generalization and in-context learning performance.

dose_response.reference: LLM judge bias amplifying in quality scores enables curriculum learning that prioritizes style-similar sequences, exposing researchers to an ordering that overfits to narrow style, harming performance on diverse tasks.
dose_response.generated: LLM quality ratings amplifying biases in data distribution, when used for curriculum learning ordering, cause clustering of similar sequences without diversity constraints, yielding homogenized training that degrades model generalization and in-context learning.
